352, "height": 480, "content": "# 📥 AUTOMATIC EMAIL DETECTION

Gmail Trigger checks inbox every minute.

## Current Settings:
- **Polling**: Every 60 seconds
- **Looks for**: New unread emails
- **Processes**: One at a time

## Optimization Tips:
- **High volume**: Keep 1 minute
- **Medium (20-50/day)**: 5 minutes
- **Low (<20/day)**: 15 minutes

💡 **Note**: Each execution uses API calls."}, "typeVersion": 1}, {"id": "d3e65c11-555b-435d-b4f3-2aa83c2cabc6", "name": "Sticky Note2", "type": "n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote", "position": [400, -64], "parameters": {"width": 880, "height": 592, "content": "# 📧 EISENHOWER MATRIX EMAIL AUTOMATION

This workflow automatically categorizes your Gmail emails using the Eisenhower Matrix productivity framework.

## THE FOUR QUADRANTS:
- **🔴 Urgent + Important (DO NOW)**: Crisis, deadlines, emergencies
- **🟢 Not Urgent + Important (SCHEDULE)**: Planning, relationships, growth
- **🟡 Urgent + Not Important (DELEGATE)**: Interruptions, some emails
- **⚫ Not Urgent + Not Important (DELETE)**: Spam, time wasters

## HOW IT WORKS:
1. New emails trigger the workflow
2. AI analyzes content and context
3. Emails get labeled automatically
4. Low-priority emails are archived

## REQUIREMENTS:
- Gmail account with OAuth2
- OpenAI API key (GPT-4)
- Four Gmail labels created"}, "typeVersion": 1}, {"id": "1e5e4e33-579f-4cd1-8aff-f1cf46db7269", "name": "Sticky Note3", "type": "n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote", "position": [976, 1184], "parameters": {"width": 416, "height": 944, "content": "# 🤖 AI CLASSIFICATION ENGINE

## Analyzes:
- Email content
- Sender importance
- Urgency keywords
- Business impact
- Deadlines

## EXAMPLES:

### 🔴 Urgent + Important:
- Legal notice requiring response
- Server down affecting customers
- Investor meeting tomorrow
- Compliance audit deadline

### 🟢 Not Urgent + Important:
- Q2 strategic planning
- Partnership opportunity
- Team development program
- Annual review prep

### 🟡 Urgent + Not Important:
- Approve expense report
- Meeting rescheduled
- Update password reminder
- Office supplies order

### ⚫ Not Urgent + Not Important:
- Newsletters
- Promotions
- Social notifications
- Marketing emails"}, "typeVersion": 1}, {"id": "428158f8-3354-49a2-a558-dc95f529e330", "name": "Sticky Note4", "type": "n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote", "position": [1680, 208], "parameters": {"width": 624, "height": 752, "content": "# 🏷️ GMAIL LABELING SYSTEM

⚠️ **IMPORTANT: Replace Label IDs!**
Each Gmail has unique IDs.
You MUST update them.

## How Categories Are Handled:

### 🔴 Urgent + Important:
- Gets red label
- Stays in inbox
- Consider notifications

### 🟢 Not Urgent + Important:
- Gets green label
- Stays in inbox
- Review in planning time

### 🟡 Urgent + Not Important:
- Gets yellow label
- Stays in inbox
- Delegate or quick reply

### ⚫ Not Urgent + Not Important:
- Gets gray label
- Auto-archived
- Bulk review later

**Pro Tip**: Create Gmail filters to show labels in sidebar."}, "typeVersion": 1}, {"id": "53971ed2-e8f7-45a5-a708-b07d663daf4f", "name": "Sticky Note5", "type": "n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote", "position": [1648, 1200], "parameters": {"width": 560, "height": 496, "content": "# 🗄️ AUTO-ARCHIVE FEATURE

✅ Removes from inbox
✅ Keeps classification label
✅ Email remains searchable
✅ Can access anytime

## Labels Being Removed:
- INBOX (archives it)
- All category labels
- Keeps custom label

## Why This Matters:
**Reduces inbox noise by 40-60%**
Lets you focus on what matters."}, "typeVersion": 1}, {"id": "9ccd31b9-f706-443e-8074-ab86ee691ebc", "name": "Sticky Note6", "type": "n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote", "position": [256, 1664], "parameters": {"width": 512, "height": 720, "content": "# 🧪 MANUAL BATCH PROCESSING

"height": 980, "content": "# 🛠️ Setup Guide
### 👤 Author: [Rakin Jakaria](https://www.youtube.com/@rakinjakaria)
### 🔌 Steps to Connect:
1. **Gmail OAuth2 Setup**  
   - Connect your Gmail account to n8n using OAuth2 authentication.
   - Go to **Credentials** in n8n and create a new **Gmail OAuth2** credential.
   - Follow the authentication flow to authorize n8n to access your Gmail account.
   - Apply this credential to all Gmail nodes (Send, Reply, Label, Delete, Get Many).

2. **Google Gemini API Key**  
   - Visit [Google AI Studio](https://aistudio.google.com/) and generate your Gemini API key.
   - Create a new **Google Gemini (PaLM) API** credential in n8n.
   - Paste your API key into the **Gemini** language model node.

3. **Google Sheets Integration**  
   - Set up **Google Sheets OAuth2** credentials in n8n.
   - Create or connect to your existing Google Sheets document for contact management.
   - Update the **Sheet** node with your specific spreadsheet ID and sheet name.
   - Ensure your sheet has columns for names and email addresses.

4. **Chat Trigger Configuration**  
   - The **Chat Trigger** node creates a webhook URL for your Gmail agent.
   - Copy the webhook URL from the Chat Trigger node.
   - Use this URL to interact with your Gmail agent via chat interface.

5. **AI Agent System Prompt Customization**  
   - Modify the system message in the **AI Agent** node to match your specific needs.
   - Update the predefined labels (youtube-viewers, sales-inquiry, etc.) to your Gmail labels.
   - Customize the assistant's name and behavior instructions.

6. **Memory Configuration**  
   - The **Memory** node stores conversation context.
   - Adjust the buffer window size based on your chat length requirements.

---
💡 **Pro Tips:**
- Test each Gmail operation individually before running the full workflow.
- Create Gmail labels beforehand for better email organization.
- Monitor your Google API usage to avoid rate limits.

Need help? Check out Rakin's [YouTube Tutorials](https://www.youtube.com/@rakinjakaria) for detailed setup instructions."}, "typeVersion": 1}, {"id": "fc096be2-2970-4170-badd-e98cab6feb5d", "name": "Sticky Note3", "type": "n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote", "position": [656, -304], "parameters": {"color": 4, "width": 480, "height": 448, "content": "## Start here: Step-by Step Youtube Tutorial :star:

請根據以下需求描述，生成一個節點與連線皆無缺失且能成功執行的 n8n workflow JSON。

需求描述：我想要設計一個自動化的機制，主要是為了讓我們的團隊每天都能收到最新的新聞摘要。這個流程需要從 BrowserAI 收集相關的新聞資訊，然後通過電子郵件或 Telegram 發送給團隊成員。理想的情況是，每天早上 8 點自動運行，同時需要有一個判斷邏輯，確保所有新聞都成功收集後再進行發送。如果收集過程中出現問題，希望能夠自動重試，直到任務完成為止。這樣一來，我們就可以節省時間，並確保團隊能夠隨時掌握最新的行業動態。
請根據以下需求描述，生成一個節點與連線皆無缺失且能成功執行的 n8n workflow JSON。

需求描述：我想要設計一個流程，幫我在每天晚上九點自動確認我的心情。這個流程需要透過Telegram發送兩個選項的按鈕，讓我可以快速選擇「我很疲倦」或「我感覺很好」。根據我的選擇，系統會自動給我一些個性化的內容，比如當我選擇疲倦時提供五分鐘的冥想，或者當我感覺良好時給我慶祝的提示。此外，這個流程還要將我的心情和反思記錄到Notion裡，並且發送一封晚安郵件，裡面包含明天的正向肯定。這樣不僅能幫助我放鬆心情，還能建立晚間的儀式感。

{"meta": {"template": {"author": "The Workflow Muse", "credits": "Design & copy: The Workflow Muse — SheCodesFlow", "subTitle": "Graceful 9PM wind‑down: Telegram mood tap → Notion log → goodnight email", "categories": ["Wellness", "Lifestyle", "Notion", "Telegram", "Email"], "description": "## 🌸 The Quiet Evening Ritual — Wind‑Down Automation Flow
**SEO Title:** The Quiet Evening Ritual — Wind‑Down Automation (Telegram + Notion + Email)  
**Meta Description:** A graceful 9PM wind‑down routine for moms & creators: Telegram mood check, personalized content, Notion journal log, and a goodnight email with tomorrow’s affirmation.

### What it does
- 9:00 PM daily ping on Telegram: *“🌙 Time to wind down, sweet soul. How was your day?”*
- User taps a mood button → *Tired* or *Great*
- If **Tired** → sends a 5‑min meditation link + (optional) AI reflection prompt
- If **Great** → celebrates the win + logs to Notion
- Logs the entry to **Notion: Evening Reflection Log**
- Sends a **Goodnight email** with tomorrow’s morning affirmation ✨

### Why it’s fresh
Not just journaling—this is a *ritualized transition* from work to rest, with warmth and automation.

## 🔧 Setup Checklist (n8n Template Requirements)
- Create credentials (no hardcoded keys):
  - **Telegram** Bot (BotFather) → add to a private chat with you
  - **Notion** (integration + database access)
  - **Email** (Gmail OAuth or SMTP)
- Make a Notion database named **Evening Reflection Log** with properties:
  - **Name** (Title), **Mood** (Select), **Reflection** (Text), **Top Win** (Text), **Date** (Date), **Affirmation** (Text)
- Import the provided CSV to auto‑create properties if needed.
- Edit the **Set: User Config** node (email, db id, links, toggles).
- Optional: Enable the GPT node by flipping `gpt_enabled` to `true` and attaching OpenAI credentials.

## ✅ n8n Submission Compliance
- Uses **Sticky Notes** with H2 markdown ✅
- **No API keys** hardcoded in nodes ✅
- Clear setup steps + credentials ✅
- Descriptive, on‑brand copy with emojis ✅

{{ $json.body.searchQuery }}", "options": {"systemMessage": "=GOAL

You are responsible for handling only the last request in the call transcription. Ignore everything before it.
Your mission now has three possible goals (in this order of priority):

Answer and guide using the database: provide short, simple answers using information found via the Google Sheets node “Get Knowledgebase” (one call max per request).

Check the available time.

Book the appointment.

You must never book an appointment before confirming availability with the client.
All appointments must be scheduled strictly in Paris time (Europe/Paris timezone).

SECURE TOOLS YOU HAVE ACCESS TO

think → You MUST use this to think carefully about how to handle the provided request data. This tool must be used on every turn and tool call interaction.

Get Knowledgebase → Reads the Knowledgebase from Google Sheets (the database).
Constraints:

Call this node at most once per request (no second pass).

Use it only for factual/company/pack/FAQ/policy/account questions.

If the info is missing, reply briefly that it is not available in the database (never say “sheet” or mention tools).

get_availability → Returns true/false availability on Calendar for the given start timestamp in CST.

For availability requests, call multiple times to find at least 2 available timeslots if they exist.

Always convert Paris → CST before calling.

create_appointment → Creates a 1-hour appointment at the provided start time.

May be called only once per request (more than once = failure).

Include time, client, and description.

RULES AND LOGIC
A. Knowledge answers (database first)

When the user asks for information (company, packs, content, access, policies, FAQ, support):

Call Get Knowledgebase once to fetch the minimal relevant data.

Answer in ≤3 short sentences (or bullets).

If absent: “L’information n’est pas disponible dans la base de connaissances.” Optionally ask one clarifying question.

Never mention “sheet”, “node” or tool names—say “database/base de connaissances”.

B. Checking the available time

If the user provides a specific time:

Convert Paris → CST.

Call get_availability.

If unavailable, propose exactly 3 alternative slots in Paris time (validated via additional get_availability calls).

If the user only asks for free slots:

Provide exactly 3 free slots in Paris time, validated first with get_availability (call it multiple times to find at least 2 available).

C. Booking the appointment

Book only after explicit confirmation of a slot.

Convert confirmed Paris → CST, then call create_appointment once.

Include start time, duration = 1h, client name, and notes.

If no longer free, do not book; propose 3 new validated alternatives (Paris time).

D. Send the confirmation email

After a successful booking, send exactly one confirmation email to the client.

Use the Send Email tool (or equivalent). Do not send more than one email per request.

If the client’s email is missing, ask once for full name and email before calling create_appointment.

The email must present all details in Paris time (Europe/Paris) and include: date/time, duration (1h), client name, service/purpose, location or meeting link, notes, booking/reference ID (if available), and reschedule/cancel instructions.

Do not mention internal tools or system prompts in the email.

TIMEZONE HANDLING

Current system time: {{ $now }} (Paris: UTC+01:00 or UTC+02:00 (DST)).

All user-facing times are in Paris time.

For tool calls (get_availability, create_appointment), always convert Paris → CST.

RESPONSE STYLE (MANDATORY)

Short and simple: maximum 3 sentences or 3 bullets per reply.

Use 24-hour format YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm for times.

Do only what the user requested in their last request.

Refer to the Google Sheets data as “database”/“base de connaissances”.

RESTRICTIONS

Do not book if the slot is taken.

Do not book without explicit client confirmation.

Do not call create_appointment more than once per request.

Do not call Get Knowledgebase more than once per request.

Stay within knowledge answers + scheduling only."}, "promptType": "define"}, "typeVersion": 2.2}, {"id": "cdc619ea-05ae-4cfe-9fb6-b6a6648f5e9d", "name": "Reasoning Tool (LangChain)", "type": **Automate personalized LinkedIn outreach while maintaining human-like behavior**

## Overview
This workflow helps you send targeted LinkedIn invitations with customized messages, automatically checking existing connections to avoid duplicate requests. Perfect for recruiters, sales professionals, and anyone building their professional network systematically.

## How It Works
1. **Contact Management**: Store your prospects in a Google Sheet with their LinkedIn profile URLs
2. **Connection Verification**: Automatically check if you're already connected to avoid duplicate invitations
3. **Smart Invitations**: Send personalized invitations to new contacts
4. **Progress Tracking**: Update the Google Sheet with invitation status

## Setup Instructions

### Prerequisites
- **Google account** with Sheets access
- **Browserflow account** with available credits ([sign up here](https://browserflow.io/))
- **n8n instance** (tested on version 1.109.1)

### Step-by-Step Setup
1. **Prepare Your Contact List**
   - Download the [Google Sheet Template](https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1mFKp3wmbV9qp2tpGGsN72zdiC32y8H1nhjdgP885y-U/edit?usp=sharing)
   - Make a copy to your Google Drive
   - Extract the Sheet ID from your URL (the string between `/d/` and `/edit`)

2. **Configure Workflow Settings**
   - Open the `settings` node and enter your Google Sheet ID
   - Customize your invitation message in the `message` node
   - Set up your Google Sheets credentials
   - Configure your Browserflow credentials

3. **Populate Your Data**
   - Add contacts to your Google Sheet with their LinkedIn profile URLs

4. **Test & Deploy**
   - Run a test with 1-2 contacts first (update the `Limit` node)
   - Monitor execution and adjust `Wait` if needed 

## Important Considerations

- **Responsible Usage**: This tool mimics human behavior and respects LinkedIn's natural usage patterns. It's designed for quality networking, not mass spamming.

- **Rate Limits**: Stay within LinkedIn's acceptable limits.

- **Account Safety**: Excessive automation can result in LinkedIn restrictions. Always prioritize authentic, valuable connections.


## Support & Community

Need assistance? Here's how to get help:
- **LinkedIn Discussion**: Comment on this [workflow post](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/n8n-about_n8n-browserflow-activity-7368758690025320448-zupZ/)
- **Direct Contact**: Connect with me on [LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/in/stephaneheckel/)
- **Community Forum**: Ask questions in the [n8n Community](https://community.n8n.io/)

**Context & Quality Principles**

* Clarify the topic before writing; if anything is unclear, ask follow-up questions first.
* Write **only in active voice**.
* Fact-check every statement; avoid ambiguity.
* Keep the tone professional, direct, and slightly conversational (not stiff, not overly casual).
* Do **not** use these words or phrases: meticulous, meticulously, navigating, complexities, realm, bespoke, tailored, towards, underpins, everchanging, ever-evolving, the world of, not only, seeking more than just, designed to enhance, it’s not merely, our suite, it is advisable, daunting, dive into, unique, ensure, utmost, in the heart of, when it comes to, in the realm of, amongst, unlock the secrets, unveil the secrets, robust.
* If the topic is highly technical, adopt a developer-centric voice.
* Aim for very high readability: varied sentence lengths, mostly short, crisp lines.
* Cut all fluff; every sentence must add value.
* Sprinkle **1–2 engagement cues mid-post** (e.g., “Ever faced this?” “Would you have done the same?”).
* Encourage reflection and conversation; posts should feel like they invite community input.

**Output Requirements**

1. Start with 2–3 **hook variations** for the first line. The main post should pick the best one, but show the alternates.
2. Write 3–5 short paragraphs that are concise, engaging, and rich in practical insights or reflections that people can apply or debate.
3. End with a thought-provoking or community-driven question that drives comments.
4. Add 4–6 relevant hashtags that balance **broad reach (e.g., #AI, #Startups)** with **niche visibility** (#TechnicalWriting, #DecisionAutomation).
5. Use 2–4 natural, well-placed emojis to break monotony and add emphasis.
6. Keep total length under 2500 characters (shorter posts often perform better).

**Formatting Rules**

* Do **not** use double quotes, special characters, or Markdown symbols in the post text.
* Escape the text so it is JSON-compatible.
* Provide the output in JSON format with fields: `hook_variations`, `post`, `hashtags`.

**Post Input**

Your job is to generate thought-leadership posts for a technical writer that maximize impressions, engagement, and community interaction.

## **Principles to Follow**

* Always clarify the topic first if unclear.
* Write only in **active voice**.
* Fact-check every statement. Avoid vague or unverified claims.
* Maintain a tone that is professional, direct, and slightly conversational. Never stiff, never overly casual.
* If the topic is technical, adopt a **developer-centric voice**.
* Keep posts highly readable: mix sentence lengths, but favor short, crisp lines.
* Eliminate fluff: every sentence must provide value.
* Include 1–2 **engagement cues mid-post** (e.g., Ever faced this? Would you have done the same?).
* Posts should encourage reflection and conversation, not just broadcasting.

## **Banned Words & Phrases**

Do not use:
meticulous, meticulously, navigating, complexities, realm, bespoke, tailored, towards, underpins, everchanging, ever-evolving, the world of, not only, seeking more than just, designed to enhance, it’s not merely, our suite, it is advisable, daunting, dive into, unique, ensure, utmost, in the heart of, when it comes to, amongst, unlock the secrets, unveil the secrets, robust.

## **Output Rules**

1. Provide 2–3 strong **hook variations** for the opening line.
2. Write the post in **3–5 short paragraphs**. Each must be concise, engaging, and packed with practical insights or reflections.
3. End with a **thought-provoking, community-driven question** that invites comments.
4. Add 4–6 relevant hashtags: balance broad reach (#AI, #Startups) with niche visibility (#TechnicalWriting, #DecisionAutomation).
5. Use 2–4 natural emojis to add emphasis without overloading the text.
6. Keep the post under **2500 characters**.

## **Formatting Rules**

* Do not use quotes, special characters, or Markdown formatting inside the post.
* Escape all text so it is JSON-compatible.
* Final output must be in strict **JSON format** with the following fields:

```json
{
  "hook_variations": [],
  "post": "",
  "hashtags": []
}
```

## Step-by-step

1. **Schedule Trigger**
   Runs on **Mon/Wed/Fri at 09:30**.
2. **Fetch pending rows (Google Sheets → Get Rows)**
   Reads the sheet and filters rows where **Status = Pending**.
3. **Limit**
   Keeps only the first pending row so one post goes out per run.
4. **Writing the post (Agent + OpenAI Chat Model + Structured Output Parser)**
   Uses **Post title** (and optional `Notes`/`Hashtags`) as input. The agent returns JSON with a `post` field. Model set to **gpt-4o-mini** by default.
5. **Create a post (LinkedIn)**
   Publishes `{{$json.output.post}}` to the configured **person** (your profile URN).
6. **Update the sheet (Google Sheets → Update)**
   Matches by `id`, sets **Status = Done**, and writes the generated text into **Output post** (or your existing output column).

## Customization

* **Schedule** — change days/time in the Schedule node. Consider your n8n server timezone.
* **Posts per run** — remove or raise the **Limit** to post more than one item.
* **Style and tone** — edit the Agent’s system prompt. Add rules for line breaks, hashtags, or a closing CTA.
* **Hashtags handling** — parse the `Hashtags` column in the prompt so the model appends them cleanly.
* **Media posts** — add a branch that attaches `Image URL` (requires LinkedIn media upload endpoints).
* **Company Page** — switch the **person** field to an **organization** URN tied to your LinkedIn app scope.

## Troubleshooting

* **No post created**

  * Check the **If/Limit** path: is there any row with `Status = Pending`?
  * Confirm the sheet **ID** and **tab name** in the Google Sheets nodes.
* **Sheet not updating**

  * The **Update** node must receive the original `id`. If you changed field names, remap them.
  * Make sure `id` values are unique.
* **LinkedIn errors (403/401/404)**

  * Refresh **LinkedIn OAuth2** in Credentials.
  * The **person/organization URN** is wrong or missing. Copy the exact URN from the LinkedIn node helper.
  * App lacks required permissions for posting.
* **Rate limit (429) or model errors**

  * Add a short **Wait** before retries.
  * Switch to a lighter model or simplify the prompt.
* **Post too long or broken formatting**

  * LinkedIn hard limit is \~3,000 characters. Add a truncation step in Code or instruct the prompt to cap length.
  * Replace double line breaks in the LinkedIn node if you see odd spacing.
* **Timezone mismatch**

  * The Schedule node uses the n8n instance timezone. Adjust or move to a Cron with explicit TZ if needed.

Need to post at a different cadence, or push two posts per day? Tweak the **Schedule** and **Limit** nodes and you’re set.

{"id": "0f715abf-ae0a-42d5-8647-5e9488cd3f9d", "name": "NOTE — Overview", "type": "n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote", "position": [400, 448], "parameters": {"color": 3, "width": 480, "height": 336, "content": "## Overview
This workflow integrates **BananaAPI.com** with the **Google Nano Banana** image generation engine. Users can submit prompts and optional reference images via a form, then the workflow calls BananaAPI.com, checks the task status in a loop, and finally returns the generated image link.

**Why use it?**
- Cost effective: only **$0.025 per image**
- **Pay-as-you-go**: pay only for what you use
- **Credits never expire**: no monthly subscription pressure

Ideal for creators, marketers, and developers who want affordable and automated image generation."}, "typeVersion": 1}, {"id": "71c558a9-d4e5-4ba2-8dc1-170e8a4ba0db", "name": "NOTE — Prerequisites", "type": "n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote", "position": [880, 448], "parameters": {"color": 3, "width": 480, "height": 336, "content": "## Prerequisites
- **BananaAPI.com account + API key (Bearer)** — create your key at https://bananaapi.com/api
- An **n8n instance** (Cloud or self-hosted)
- Basic knowledge of AI prompts to get the best quality images

⚠️ Never commit or share your API key publicly. Use **n8n Credentials** for production setups."}, "typeVersion": 1}, {"id": "77a8c9f2-61af-4e2e-9ba4-6c3ca1494567", "name": "NOTE — Step 1 (Form Input)", "type": "n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote", "position": [400, 800], "parameters": {"width": 464, "height": 496, "content": "## Step 1 — User Form Input
The **Form Trigger** asks the user to provide:
- `api_token` (required) — Banana API Bearer YOUR_TOKEN_HERE
- `prompt` (required) — image description (e.g. *“a neon cyberpunk cat, detailed, 4k”*)
- `Output Format [optional]` — choose PNG or JPEG
- `Image Size [optional]` — 16:9, 9:16, 1:1, 3:4, 4:3
- `image_url_1 ... image_url_5 [optional]` — reference images for editing or transformation"}, "typeVersion": 1}, {"id": "5ae4ce78-4f6c-4148-8485-a90d21ba32dd", "name": "NOTE — Step 2 (POST Request)", "type": "n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote", "position": [896, 800], "parameters": {"width": 464, "height": 496, "content": "## Step 2 — Submit to Banana API
The **HTTP POST** node sends the request:
- URL: `https://bananaapi.com/api/n8n/generate/`
- Headers:
  - `Content-Type: application/json`
  - `Authorization: Bearer {{ $json.api_token }}`
- JSON Body includes:
  - `prompt`
  - `image_size`
  - `output_format`
  - `image_urls` (array of up to 5)

BananaAPI will return a `taskId` to track progress."}, "typeVersion": 1}, {"id": "3c7bfc47-5d0f-45fb-a53f-25c24d2ed1f5", "name": "NOTE — Step 3 (Processing)", "type": "n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote", "position": [1392, 800], "parameters": {"width": 912, "height": 496, "content": "## Step 3 — Processing
BananaAPI forwards the request to the **Nano Banana** engine, which generates or edits the image based on the prompt and optional input images.

⏱ Average generation time: a few seconds to ~1 minute, depending on complexity and server load.
- After 5s wait, the workflow calls:
  `GET https://bananaapi.com/api/n8n/image-status/{{ taskId }}`
- If `status != completed`, the loop waits 5s and retries
- When `status = completed`, the workflow continues"}, "typeVersion": 1}, {"id": "583222b7-f53c-4376-8958-4c544c4ace60", "name": "NOTE — Step 5 (Output)", "type": "n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote", "position": [2336, 800], "parameters": {"width": 464, "height": 496, "content": "## Step 4 — Return Output
When completed, the **Set node** outputs:
- `image_url` — the final generated image link
- `task_id` — the task reference ID
- `status` — task completion state

💡 Tip: Add a **Webhook Response** node to return a clean JSON payload for frontend apps."}, "typeVersion": 1}, {"id": "abfd4d9f-d7be-4a74-87ae-8618a6ff826a", "name": "NOTE — API Reference", "type": "n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote", "position": [2320, 448], "parameters": {"color": 3, "width": 480, "height": 336, "content": "## API Reference
- **POST** `https://bananaapi.com/api/n8n/generate/`
- **GET** `https://bananaapi.com/api/n8n/image-status/{taskId}`

Docs: https://bananaapi.com/api

✅ Always include `Authorization: Bearer <token>` in headers."}, "typeVersion": 1}, {"id": "5074a457-5ec7-4a5c-81bc-54c120818b08", "name": "NOTE — Pricing Advantages", "type": "n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote", "position": [1360, 448], "parameters": {"color": 3, "width": 480, "height": 336, "content": "## Pricing Advantages
- **Only $0.025 per image** — very cost-effective
- **Pay-as-you-go**: no monthly subscription
- **Credits never expire** — use anytime, no rush

Perfect for scaling automation workflows or experimenting without wasted costs."}, "typeVersion": 1}, {"id": "1d11806a-56e9-4e02-9aeb-5dfef750b1ae", "name": "NOTE — Troubleshooting", "type": "n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote", "position": [1840, 448], "parameters": {"color": 3, "width": 480, "height": 336, "content": "## Troubleshooting
- **401/403 Unauthorized** → Check Authorization header (`Bearer YOUR_TOKEN_HERE`)
- **Invalid JSON** → Ensure POST body is valid JSON (double quotes, no trailing commas)
- **No imageUrl returned** → Task still pending; wait longer or re-check taskId
- **Slow performance** → Increase wait interval from 5s to 8–10s"}, "typeVersion": 1}], "pinData": {}, "connections": {"If": {"main": [[{"node": "Return Links", "type": "main", "index": 0}], [{"node": "Wait 5s (loop)", "type": "main", "index": 0}]]}, "Wait 5s": {"main": [[{"node": "Check Status", "type": "main", "index": 0}]]}, "Check Status": {"main": [[{"node": "If", "type": "main", "index": 0}]]}, "Wait 5s (loop)": {"main": [[{"node": "Check Status", "type": "main", "index": 0}]]}, "Form — Get Prompt": {"main": [[{"node": "Submit — Banana API", "type": "main", "index": 0}]]}, "Submit — Banana API": {"main": [[{"node": "Wait 5s", "type": "main", "index": 0}]]}}}

需求描述：我想要建立一個流程來記錄我們系統中的重要事件。當某些操作發生時，例如用戶資料更新或錯誤發生，我希望自動將這些信息紀錄到 Supabase 的資料庫中。具體來說，我需要能夠記錄下工作流程的名稱、執行的節點，以及事件的嚴重程度（像是 DEBUG、INFO 或 ERROR），還有一段描述性的訊息。這樣我們就能夠更有效地追蹤系統的運行狀況，並且在出現問題時能夠迅速找到根源。希望這個自動化流程能夠幫助我們提升工作效率，並且減少手動紀錄的錯誤。

{"id": "0twZGM6kPlG2rFsv", "meta": {"instanceId": "313b93d489cbc0ae87ad93f25c395bbdc59c46c632d00f395c3586a8fbbb7a34", "templateCredsSetupCompleted": true}, "name": "TEMPL - Logger Sub-Workflow", "tags": [], "nodes": [{"id": "d8485aa8-429a-4eca-804b-2e2b5ed1a61e", "name": "Sticky Note", "type": "n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote", "position": [-784, -48], "parameters": {"color": 4, "width": 816, "height": 1248, "content": "## Centralized Logging with Supabase (Log4j2-style)


This workflow provides a simple yet powerful logging solution for n8n, inspired by Log4j2's log levels. 

It enables you to send structured log entries (DEBUG, INFO, WARN, ERROR, FATAL) directly to a Supabase PostgreSQL database. Whether you're tracking workflow executions, node-level issues, or custom debugging events, this template offers a clean and scalable way to store and query logs from across your automation stack — perfect for monitoring, debugging, or building analytics dashboards.


You need first to setup Supabase database and credentials:


### 1. Supabase Configuration

- Create an enumerated type (in Database -> Database Management -> Enumerated Types):
	- Name: **log_level_type**
	- Values: TRACE, DEBUG, INFO, WARN, ERROR, FATAL


- Create a table **logs** with following fields  (in Database -> Database Management -> Tables):
	- id #int8 (leave by default)
	- created_at timestamp (leave by default)
	- workflow_name text (deactivate the "Is Nullable" option)
	- node_name text (deactivate the "Is Nullable" option)
	- execution_id text (deactivate the "Is Nullable" option)
	- log_level log_level_type (deactivate the "Is Nullable" option)
	- message text (deactivate the "Is Nullable" option)
  	- metadata json


- If you prefer the SQL scripts:

```
-- Enum type for log_level
create type log_level_type as enum ('TRACE','DEBUG','INFO','WARN','ERROR','FATAL');
  
-- logs table
create table publi c.logs (
  id bigserial primary key,
  created_at timestamp with time zone default now() not null,
  workflow_name text not null,
  node_name text not null,
  execution_id text not null,
  log_level log_level_type not null,
  message text not null,
  metadata jsonb
);
```



### 2. Create Credentials to your Supabase Account in N8N

https://docs.n8n.io/integrations/builtin/credentials/supabase/

{"meta": {"instanceId": "47d295a7329d3547e0e6b56de9e439dc1a0a49c6d827b2320629f54300c47e41"}, "nodes": [{"id": "15ab6203-2c77-45ac-b5dd-79f404e090fa", "name": "Sticky Note", "type": "n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote", "position": [0, -140], "parameters": {"width": 1220, "height": 300, "content": "

##  🡫 How To Get Started 

1. Double-click the **APImage API** node to open it.
2. Replace "_YOUR_API_KEY_" with your API Key.
3. Use the **Replace Background** node to submit a request.

Note: You can find your API Key inside the Dashboard of your APImage account. 

[Open the Dashboard 🡥](https://apimage.org/dashboard) 

Need support with your API key? ✉ [ask@support.apimage.org](mailto:ask@support.apimage.org)"}, "typeVersion": 1}, {"id": "8cb25cf6-2de1-4a8a-b39f-dd3d277ef96e", "name": "Sticky Note2", "type": "n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote", "position": [920, 180], "parameters": {"color": 4, "width": 300, "height": 1160, "content": "### Output Images  

You can store or upload the processed images to any platform of your choice.  






















Simply connect nodes for the service where you want the images saved, for example:  

- SQLite, MySQL, PostgreSQL, and other databases  
- Google Drive, Dropbox, OneDrive, or Box  
- S3 object storage, FTP servers, or WebDAV  
- Airtable, Notion, Coda, or Smartsheet  
- And many other services  

Be sure to place these nodes **after the "Download Image" node**.  
By default, the output image files are named **`data`**. "}, "typeVersion": 1}, {"id": "1024a4b1-eecb-4fbd-8740-5ce45f238df0", "name": "Sticky Note1", "type": "n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote", "position": [0, 180], "parameters": {"color": 4, "width": 280, "height": 1160, "content": "### Input Image  

The **Replace Background** node can be substituted with any service that provides an input image URL along with a description of the desired background.





















You can either use the **Form node** to manually enter image URLs for background removal, or connect nodes to any data source that provides image links, for example:

- SQLite, MySQL, PostgreSQL, and other databases  
- Google Drive, Dropbox, OneDrive, Box, and similar storage services  
- S3 object storage, FTP servers, or WebDAV  
- Airtable, Notion, Coda, or Smartsheet  
- Google Sheets, Excel Online, or CSV files  
- CRM systems like HubSpot, Pipedrive, or Salesforce  
- CMS platforms like WordPress, Strapi, or Contentful  
- Or virtually any other service that supplies image URLs"}, "typeVersion": 1}, {"id": "daaec5d9-342d-4f2f-85d2-0ca1a072dd4c", "name": "Download Image", "type": "n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest", "position": [700, 380], "parameters": {"url": "={{ $json.data.url }}", "options": {}}, "typeVersion": 4.2}, {"id": "3f76cef0-807e-42e7-9a6f-4d078e86aada", "name": "APImage API", "type": "n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest", "position": [400, 380], "parameters": {"url": "https://apimage.org/api/ai-replace-background", "method": "POST", "options": {}, "sendBody": true, "sendHeaders": true, "bodyParameters": {"parameters": [{"name": "image_url", "value": "={{ $json["Image URL"] }}"}, {"name": "background_prompt", "value": "={{ $json["Background Description"] }}"}, {"name": "preserve_subject", "value": "0.8"}, {"name": "light_direction", "value": "above"}, {"name": "light_strength", "value": "0.6"}, {"name": "output_format", "value": "png"}]}, "headerParameters": {"parameters": [{"name": "Authorization", "value": "Bearer YOUR_API_KEY_HERE"}]}}, "typeVersion": 4.2}, {"id": "ad8c0911-3d2b-4acb-ad4c-8b96e556e0d0", "name": "Sticky Note3", "type": "n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote", "position": [300, 180], "parameters": {"color": 4, "width": 280, "height": 460, "content": "### Replace + Relight Background

This is where the image processing happens. **APImage** removes the original background, applies the new one, and optimizes the overall lighting for a natural look.  

















Open the **APImage API** node above and replace "_YOUR_API_KEY_" with your API Key. [Get your API Key here 🡥](https://apimage.org/dashboard) 

"}, "typeVersion": 1}, {"id": "ad068051-dcbe-49f9-8345-f42f1edc754c", "name": "Sticky Note4", "type": "n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote", "position": [600, 180], "parameters": {"color": 4, "width": 300, "height": 460, "content": "### Download Image  

This step downloads the processed image and makes it available for storage, sharing, or further processing.  
"}, "typeVersion": 1}, {"id": "07ef7110-3d9e-4773-96f2-6f6c2143a012", "name": "Sticky Note5", "type": "n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote", "position": [300, 660], "parameters": {"width": 600, "height": 680, "content": "### Fequently Asked Questions 

**Q: How is background replacement different from background removal?**  
A: Background removal makes the background transparent, while background replacement swaps it with a new one and adjusts lighting for a natural look.

**Q: What types of images are supported?**  
A: APImage works with standard image formats such as JPG, PNG, and WebP.  

**Q: Can I choose a custom background?**  
A: Yes. You can provide a description of the desired background, or integrate APImage into workflows that supply predefined background images.  

**Q: What is the default filename for processed images?**  
A: The default filename is **`data`**, unless you rename the file in your workflow.  

**Q: Do I need to store the images locally?**  
A: No. You can connect APImage to any supported service (databases, cloud storage, CMS platforms, APIs, etc.) and store images directly.  

**Q: Can APImage be used in automated workflows?**  
A: Absolutely. It integrates with n8n and can process images automatically from sources like databases, cloud storage, or APIs.  

**Q: Does it also enhance image quality?**  
A: APImage adjusts lighting and contrast to make background replacements look natural, but it does not perform full image upscaling.  

**Q: Is there a limit to the number of images I can process?**  
A: Processing limits depend on your subscription plan and allocated credits.

This workflow automates the extraction and presentation of hotel data from Booking.com. It is triggered by a chat message and uses the Bright Data API to scrape real-time information.

👉 Get your free Bright Data API key here: [Bright Data](https://get.brightdata.com/unstoppable)

## Key Features
Bright Data Integration: Utilizes Bright Data's Web Scraper to extract hotel listings from Booking.com based on a user-provided city.

AI-Powered Data Refinement: Leverages an OpenRouter AI model to clean and format the extracted data into a user-friendly list.

Calculations: Employs a Calculator tool to convert prices and ensure a clear, actionable output.

Dynamic Response: The final result is a well-presented, synthesized list of hotels, ready for immediate use.

[Phil | Inforeole](https://inforeole.fr)

======================================="}, "typeVersion": 1}, {"id": "22f27f0a-4405-4584-83a3-65129c738032", "name": "Sticky Note4", "type": "n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote", "position": [-1920, 288], "parameters": {"color": 4, "width": 589, "height": 1958, "content": "## 🎬 **WAN-VIDEO 2.5 - IMAGE TO VIDEO WORKFLOW**

**🔥 Powered by Replicate API and n8n Automation**

---

### 📝 **Model Overview**

- **Owner**: wan-video
- **Model**: wan-2.5-i2v
- **Type**: Image to Video Generation
- **API Endpoint**: https://api.replicate.com/v1/models/wan-video/wan-2.5-i2v/predictions

**🎯 What This Model Does:**
Transforms static images into dynamic videos using AI. Perfect for creating promotional videos, social media content, and animated presentations from single images.

---

### 📋 **Parameter Reference**

**🔴 Required Parameters:** 
- **image** (string): Input image URL to animate
- **prompt** (string): Description of the desired video motion and action

**🔵 Optional Parameters:** 
- **seed** (integer): Random seed for reproducible generation
- **num_frames** (integer): Number of frames to generate (affects video length)
- **guidance_scale** (number): How closely to follow the prompt (7-15 typical)
- **num_inference_steps** (integer): Quality vs speed tradeoff (20-50 typical)
- *Additional parameters available in API documentation*

---

### 🔧 **Workflow Components Explained**

**🎯 Manual Trigger**
- Starts the workflow execution
- Click to begin video generation process

**🔐 Set API Token** 
- Configures your Replicate API authentication
- Replace with your actual Replicate API token
- Essential for accessing the wan-video model

**⚙️ Add Seed Image and Prompt**
- Sets the input image URL and video prompt
- Properly formats the prompt to handle quotes
- Pre-configured with AG1 marketing video example

**🚀 Create Video**
- Sends the generation request to Replicate API
- Uses wan-video/wan-2.5-i2v model
- Returns a prediction ID for status tracking

**⏳ Wait & Status Checking Loop**
- Waits 5 seconds then checks prediction status
- Continues checking until completion or failure
- Implements intelligent retry logic with 10-second delays

**✅ Success/Error Handling**
- Routes successful completions to success response
- Handles failures gracefully with error details
- Returns structured JSON response with video URL or errors

**📊 Logging & Monitoring**
- Logs all requests for debugging and monitoring
- Tracks timestamps and prediction IDs
- Helps identify issues during development

---

### 🌟 **Key Benefits**

- **🎬 AI Video Generation**: Transform static images into engaging videos
- **📱 Perfect for Social Media**: Create dynamic content for TikTok, Instagram, YouTube
- **🔄 Automated Workflow**: Handles the complete generation pipeline automatically
- **🛡️ Error Resilience**: Built-in retry logic and comprehensive error handling
- **📈 Production Ready**: Includes logging, monitoring, and structured responses
- **⚡ Efficient Processing**: Optimized API calls with intelligent status checking

---

### 🚀 **Quick Start Instructions**

1. **🔑 Get Your API Key**
   - Sign up at https://replicate.com
   - Navigate to your account settings
   - Copy your API token

2. **🔧 Configure the Workflow**
   - Replace the API token in "Set API Token" node
   - Update image URL in "Add Seed Image and Prompt" node
   - Customize the prompt for your desired video motion

3. **▶️ Execute the Workflow**
   - Click the 'Manual Trigger' to start
   - Monitor the execution in the n8n interface
   - Check logs for detailed execution information

4. **📥 Get Your Results**
   - Successful generations return a URL to your video
   - Download the MP4 file from the provided URL
   - Video typically ready in 30-120 seconds

---

### 🔍 **Troubleshooting Guide**

**Common Issues:**
- **Invalid API Token**: Ensure your Replicate token is valid and has sufficient credits
- **Image URL Issues**: Verify the image URL is publicly accessible
- **Prompt Formatting**: Use single quotes inside prompts to avoid JSON errors
- **Generation Timeout**: Video generation can take 1-3 minutes - be patient

**Best Practices:**
- Use high-quality input images (minimum 512x512)
- Write detailed, specific prompts for better results
- Test with shorter prompts first, then add complexity
- Keep API tokens secure - use n8n credentials feature
- Monitor your Replicate usage and billing

---

### 📝 **Example Use Cases**

- **Product Demos**: Animate product images with usage demonstrations
- **Social Media Ads**: Create eye-catching video ads from product photos
- **Story Telling**: Bring illustrations to life with motion
- **Marketing Content**: Transform static campaigns into dynamic videos
- **Educational Content**: Animate diagrams and infographics

---

**🔗 Additional Resources:**
- Model Documentation: https://replicate.com/wan-video/wan-2.5-i2v
- Replicate API Docs: https://replicate.com/docs
- n8n Documentation: https://docs.n8n.io

**Option 1: Same Image + Same Prompt = Multiple Videos**
- Get different variations from identical inputs
- Example: 1 product photo → 5 unique videos

**Option 2: Same Image + Different Prompts = Multiple Videos**
- Test different animations on one image  
- Example: 1 photo with 10 different prompts

**Option 3: Different Images + Different Prompts = Multiple Videos**
- Bulk create unique content
- Example: 50 products, each with custom prompt"}, "typeVersion": 1}, {"id": "9fe706b2-a214-4165-8cd7-e1af7c3e4a41", "name": "Wait 90s", "type": "n8n-nodes-base.wait", "position": [800, 224], "webhookId": "51a05aef-e220-406d-8c2c-e59c82c3e26e", "parameters": {"unit": "seconds", "amount": 90}, "typeVersion": 1}], "pinData": {}, "connections": {"Wait 5s": {"main": [[{"node": "Check Status", "type": "main", "index": 0}]]}, "Wait 90s": {"main": [[{"node": "Check Status", "type": "main", "index": 0}]]}, "Has Failed?": {"main": [[{"node": "Error Response", "type": "main", "index": 0}], [{"node": "Wait 90s", "type": "main", "index": 0}]]}, "Log Request": {"main": [[{"node": "Wait 5s", "type": "main", "index": 0}]]}, "Check Status": {"main": [[{"node": "Is Complete?", "type": "main", "index": 0}]]}, "Create Video": {"main": [[{"node": "Log Request", "type": "main", "index": 0}]]}, "Is Complete?": {"main": [[{"node": "Success Response", "type": "main", "index": 0}], [{"node": "Has Failed?", "type": "main", "index": 0}]]}, "Set API Token": {"main": [[{"node": "Add Seed Image and Prompt", "type": "main", "index": 0}]]}, "Display Result": {"main": [[]]}, "Error Response": {"main": [[{"node": "Display Result", "type": "main", "index": 0}]]}, "Manual Trigger": {"main": [[{"node": "Set API Token", "type": "main", "index": 0}]]}, "Loop Over Items": {"main": [[], [{"node": "Loop Over Items", "type": "main", "index": 0}]]}, "Success Response": {"main": [[{"node": "Display Result", "type": "main", "index": 0}]]}, "Add Seed Image and Prompt": {"main": [[{"node": "Create Video", "type": "main", "index": 0}]]}}}

需求描述：我想要設計一個自動化的流程，目的是為了更有效率地管理來自 Stripe 的付款收據。具體來說，當收到付款後，我需要自動檢索最新的發票，檢查它們的支付狀態，並且對於已支付的發票，生成收據並通過電子郵件發送給客戶。此外，這些發票的 PDF 檔案也要自動上傳到 Google Drive 中，並且需要將收據的相關資訊記錄到 Google Sheets 中。最重要的是，這個流程還必須能夠自動記錄任何錯誤，以便我們能夠及時進行調整和修正。希望這樣的設計能夠提升我們的工作效率，減少人工操作的錯誤。

Action: Fetches invoice data from Stripe."}, "typeVersion": 1}, {"id": "1d8ec260-aa38-4dfa-8f73-98ea5e5e671c", "name": "Sticky Note1", "type": "n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote", "position": [656, -544], "parameters": {"height": 288, "content": "Send Receipt Email –

請根據以下需求描述，生成一個節點與連線皆無缺失且能成功執行的 n8n workflow JSON。

需求描述：我有一個需求，希望能透過 Telegram 建立一個智能機器人，這個機器人能夠接收不同類型的訊息，包括語音、圖片、視頻和文字。當用戶發送訊息後，機器人能自動判別訊息的類型，然後使用相應的人工智慧模型進行處理，最後將結果回覆給用戶。

這個流程需要整合 Telegram 和一些 AI 工具，比如 OpenAI 和 Google Gemini。希望在訊息處理時，能夠根據訊息的類型選擇合適的模型進行分析，讓用戶能夠獲得即時的回應。這樣的自動化流程不僅能提升效率，還能增強用戶體驗。

"name": "Code", "type": "n8n-nodes-base.code", "position": [624, 784], "parameters": {"jsCode": "// Obtener la fecha del Schedule Trigger
const triggerData = $('Schedule Trigger').first().json;
const originalDate = new Date(triggerData.scheduled || triggerData.timestamp || new Date());

// Convertir a zona horaria de Santiago de Chile
const santiagoDate = new Date(originalDate.toLocaleString("en-US", {timeZone: "America/Santiago"}));

// Arrays para traducir al español
const meses = [
  'Enero', 'Febrero', 'Marzo', 'Abril', 'Mayo', 'Junio',
  'Julio', 'Agosto', 'Septiembre', 'Octubre', 'Noviembre', 'Diciembre'
];

const diasSemana = [
  'Domingo', 'Lunes', 'Martes', 'Miércoles', 'Jueves', 'Viernes', 'Sábado'
];

// Extraer componentes de la fecha
const dia = santiagoDate.getDate();
const mes = meses[santiagoDate.getMonth()];
let horas = santiagoDate.getHours();
const ampm = horas >= 12 ? 'pm' : 'am';

// Convertir a formato de 12 horas
horas = horas % 12;
horas = horas ? horas : 12; // la hora '0' debe ser '12'

const diaSemana = diasSemana[santiagoDate.getDay()];

// Crear el formato deseado: "22 de Junio a las 9 am"
const readableTime = `${dia} de ${mes} a las ${horas} ${ampm}`;

return [{
  json: {
    "Readable time": readableTime,
    "Day of week": diaSemana
  }
}];"}, "typeVersion": 2}, {"id": "67e4c47c-6c50-4db7-980a-9f9d90c02171", "name": "Schedule Trigger", "type": "n8n-nodes-base.scheduleTrigger", "position": [64, 704], "parameters": {"rule": {"interval": [{"triggerAtHour": 9}, {"triggerAtHour": 21}]}}, "typeVersion": 1.2}, {"id": "1d12a289-2338-4c17-ae47-03eb3e3029f0", "name": "Google Sheets", "type": "n8n-nodes-base.googleSheets", "position": [368, 768], "parameters": {"options": {"returnFirstMatch": true}, "filtersUI": {"values": [{"lookupValue": "Listo para postear ", "lookupColumn": "Status"}]}, "sheetName": {"__rl": true, "mode": "list", "value": "gid=0", "cachedResultUrl": "https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1ZKbnD7GM6eAHKzyDY98cvz9jMUib1HLU7d9gohYkR3s/edit#gid=0", "cachedResultName": "Video"}, "documentId": {"__rl": true, "mode": "id", "value": "1ZKbnD7GM6eAHKzyDY98cvz9jMUib1HLU7d9gohYkR3s"}}, "credentials": {"googleSheetsOAuth2Api": {"id": "credential-id", "name": "googleSheetsOAuth2Api Credential"}}, "typeVersion": 4.6}, {"id": "b1fa7e04-f27a-4ad4-b1ea-705e5f4e0c57", "name": "Send a text message", "type": "n8n-nodes-base.telegram", "position": [2160, 736], "webhookId": "6a86891a-9444-45ed-9ff4-b20d3fc71f58", "parameters": {"text": "=**📹 Resultados de publicación:**
